title: Korean Red Beans & Rice Bowl with Bulgogi Beef

ingredients.name: 1 lb (450 g) dry red kidney beans (or small red beans)
6–8 cups water or unsalted chicken stock
2 tbsp soy sauce (plus more to finish)
1 tbsp gochugaru (optional, for cleaner chili flavor)
2 cups short-grain white rice (or medium grain)
Water per your rice cooker / package instructions
1.25 lb (560 g) thin-sliced ribeye or sirloin (bulgogi cut)
1 small Asian pear, grated (or 1 kiwi, or 1/2 apple)
1 small garlic clove, minced or grated
2 medium zucchini, thin-sliced into half moons

instruction_groups.name: 1) Prep the beans
steps: Pick through beans for stones/debris. Rinse thoroughly.

Optional soak: cover with water and soak 8–12 hours. Drain.
          (Soaking shortens cook time and improves texture.)

instruction_groups.name: 2) Start the bean base
steps: Heat a large pot over medium heat. Add neutral oil.
Add diced onion and scallion whites. Cook 6–8 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and ginger. Cook 45–60 seconds until fragrant.
Add beans and 6 cups water/stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Simmer uncovered 45–60 minutes, stirring occasionally.

instruction_groups.name: 3) Add Korean seasoning
steps: Once beans are starting to soften (after ~45–60 minutes), stir in:
2–4 tbsp gochujang
1–2 tbsp doenjang
2 tbsp soy sauce
Optional: gochugaru and sugar/honey
Continue simmering another 45–90 minutes until beans are very tender.
Add more water as needed to keep a thick-stew consistency.
2–4 tbsp gochujang
1–2 tbsp doenjang
2 tbsp soy sauce
Optional: gochugaru and sugar/honey

instruction_groups.name: 4) Thicken like Louisiana red beans
steps: When beans are fully tender, mash ~20% of them against the side of the pot.
Simmer 10 more minutes. The pot should become creamy and cohesive.
Finish with rice vinegar (or kimchi juice), black pepper, and sesame oil.
Taste and adjust salt with soy sauce.
Target consistency: thick stew that still pours. If it gets too thick, add water 1/4 cup at a time.

instruction_groups.name: 5) Cook the rice
steps: Rinse rice until water runs mostly clear (2–4 rinses).
Cook using your rice cooker or stovetop method.
Optional: fold in sesame oil + sesame seeds after cooking.

instruction_groups.name: 6) Bulgogi beef
steps: In a bowl, combine grated pear, soy sauce, sugar, mirin, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, pepper, sesame seeds.
Add beef and sliced onion. Mix thoroughly.
Marinate 30 minutes (or up to overnight).

Heat a skillet or grill pan over high heat. Cook beef in batches 2–4 minutes,
          until browned and cooked through.

instruction_groups.name: 7) Spinach namul
steps: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Salt it lightly.
Add spinach and blanch 30–60 seconds until wilted bright green.
Drain and rinse under cold water. Squeeze out as much water as possible.
Toss spinach with soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic, and sesame seeds.

instruction_groups.name: 8) Sautéed zucchini
steps: Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add neutral oil.
Add zucchini and cook 3–5 minutes until softened but not mushy.
Add garlic and salt. Cook 30–60 seconds.
Optional: finish with sesame oil, sesame seeds, and a pinch of gochugaru.
